element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns momentum and jump timing on flat terrain
Can eat enemies and spit projectiles; changes movement physics
Grants size increase for tank durability; breakable blocks only breakable with power-up
Projectile offensive capability; range-based enemy elimination
Flight and gliding mechanics; high ceiling exploration; requires aerial control proficiency
Floats upward with time limit; reaches elevated secrets
Crushes enemies; bounces off invulnerable enemies; breaks certain blocks with power-up
Navigational challenge with multiple branching paths and hazard traps
Themed miniboss encounter; gated progression to next world
Tracked in HUD; incentivizes thorough level exploration without blocking progression
Camera controls pace; player must react to forced progression rhythm
Instant death on contact; demands platforming precision
Nonlinear route selection; optional stages and secret exits create player agency

typical_tension_triggers: Contact with lava or bottomless pit
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Graduated escalation from safe exploration to hazard-dense navigation; fortress and castle encounters create punctuated tension spikes; overworld branching provides player-controlled pacing rhythm

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Dragon Coins and elevated areas accessible with Cape Feather or P-Balloon incentivize vertical exploration; branching paths on overworld create optional route visibility
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Flying above visible terrain with Cape Feather reveals hidden stages and shortcuts; secret exits typically accessed via alternative routes on overworld map

difficulty_curve.early_game: World 1 introduces mechanics in isolation: basic platforming, Yoshi, power-up collection, spin jump, ghost houses. Low enemy density; flat to gently sloped terrain; generous time limits
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Worlds 2-4 escalate: hazards compound (lava, moving platforms); auto-scrolling sections introduce forced pacing; fortress difficulty increases with multi-stage Koopaling encounters; required use of learned mechanics in combination
difficulty_curve.late_game: Worlds 5-7 demand expertise: high-density enemy encounters; complex platforming chains; Bowser's Castle finale; optional 96-exit completion requires systematic route optimization and hazard mastery
notable_spikes: First Ghost House (World 1) - introduces branching paths and disorientation
First Auto-scrolling Level (World 2+) - removes player camera control, tempo externalized
Fortress encounters - boss pattern memorization checkpoint
Bowser's Castle (World 7) - multi-stage finale with all mechanics required

notable_level_patterns.description: Early levels isolate single mechanics (Yoshi platforming, power-up collection) on safe terrain with time buffer and enemy visibility. Establishes mastery before combination challenges.
Stages layer hazards progressively: simple pits → lava chains → auto-scroll forced pacing → enemy density spikes. Each adds cognitive/mechanical load.
Each world culminates in multi-stage Koopaling fortress requiring applied mechanic mastery. Gated progression; boss pattern recognition required.
Ghost Houses and branching paths on overworld map allow player route selection. Dragon Coins and secret exits incentivize thoroughness without blocking progression.
Cape Feather enables above-terrain flight corridors revealing hidden stages and shortcuts. Spatial reward for learned flight mechanics.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Various World 1+ stages with elevated coin chains
